news.title: ASCO: Akeso’s ivonescimab bests PD-1 inhibitor in lung cancer chemo combos, slashing death risk by 34%
news.snippet: Although the first-in-class PD-1xVEGF bispecific didn’t disappoint on its overall survival headline message, detailed results from ivonescimab’s chemo-combo readout in first-line lung cancer are being

news.title: ASCO: Revolution Medicines confident in RAS leadership as rivals square up
news.snippet: This year’s meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology is abuzz with talk of RAS inhibitors, largely driven by Revolution Medicines’ barnburner pancreatic cancer data drop earlier this year.

news.title: ASCO: Lilly exec points to ‘nutty’ 100% response rate for in vivo CAR-T as justifying Kelonia buyout
news.snippet: Kelonia Therapeutics has shared the largest slice of data yet for an in vivo CAR-T, helping to justify the $3.2 billion price tag that Eli Lilly recently paid to buy the Boston biotech.

news.title: ASCO: In next-gen IO race, BioNTech/BMS and Pfizer are divided on overall survival approach
news.snippet: In the race to forge the next Keytruda from PD-(L)1xVEGF bispecific antibodies, Pfizer and the BioNTech-Bristol Myers Squibb alliance are split on how to position overall survival—the gold-standard on

news.title: ASCO: Gilead touts ADC ovarian cancer data as proof Tubulis buyout was ‘a good deal’
news.snippet: The German biotech shared updated data from a phase 1 study of the antibody-drug conjugate TUB-040, the star behind Gilead’s decision to buy the company for $3.15 billion upfront earlier this year.

news.title: Legend scientific founder returns to ASCO with new ambition for high-yield, non-gene-editing CAR-T platform
news.snippet: Nine years after wowing the audience at the American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting with a CAR-T candidate that would become Carvykti—now the world’s most successful cell therapy—Legend B

news.title: AstraZeneca, after phase 3 miss, posts subgroup data behind pitch for rare disease approval
news.snippet: AstraZeneca has shared data it believes can rescue anselamimab from a phase 3 failure, linking the rare disease drug candidate to a 62% improvement in survival in a prespecified subgroup.

news.title: Agios’ next-gen PK drug fails blood cancer study but sickle cell disease ambitions remain undimmed
news.snippet: Agios Pharmaceuticals’ next-gen pyruvate kinase (PK) activator has failed a phase 2 study in a group of rare blood cancers, although the biopharma is still holding out hope in sickle cell disease (SCD

news.title: Boston, China and the future of biotech
news.snippet: Senior Writer Darren Incorvaia talks with industry leaders and experts from Flagship Pioneering, MassBio and PassKey about funding pressures, AI, China's biotech ecosystem and what's next for drug dev

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Allogene swaps in CMO as new chief exec
news.snippet: After more than eight years at the helm of off-the-shelf CAR-T company Allogene Therapeutics, co-founder David Chang, M.D., Ph.D., is stepping down so that Zachary Roberts, M.D., Ph.D., the biotech’s

news.title: ASCO: BMS’ CELMoD drug doubles PFS in ‘incredibly exciting’ ph. 3 myeloma study
news.snippet: Bristol Myers Squibb may have gone big in its pursuit of cereblon E3 ligase modulator (CELMoD) drugs, but today the pharma is standing tall.

news.title: ASCO: Lilly debuts ‘strong’ bladder cancer data for potential Padcev challenger
news.snippet: Eli Lilly took to the stage at the American Society of Clinical Oncology meeting in Chicago to share early data for a new drug candidate that could, if all goes right, challenge the dominance of Pfize

news.title: Gilead scraps lead arthritis drug from $405M MiroBio buyout in latest blow to BTLA agonist field
news.snippet: Gilead has abandoned work on the lead rheumatoid arthritis drug from its $405 million buyout of MiroBio in the latest blow to the BTLA agonist field.

news.title: Pfizer pens $10B, 12-drug deal with Innovent in broad bet on Chinese cancer med innovation
news.snippet: Pfizer is paying Innovent $650 million upfront to partner on cancer drug development, following Bristol Myers Squibb by striking a broad pact that positions it to leverage China’s fast early developme

news.title: FDA extends Beren’s rare disease review after info request, further stretching saga
news.snippet: The FDA has delayed its approval decision on Beren Therapeutics’ rare disease drug candidate, extending the asset’s long path toward the U.S. market.

news.title: ASCO: Akeso’s ivonescimab bests PD-1 inhibitor in lung cancer chemo combos, slashing death risk by 34%
news.snippet: Although the first-in-class PD-1xVEGF bispecific didn’t disappoint on its overall survival headline message, detailed results from ivonescimab’s chemo-combo readout in first-line lung cancer are being

news.title: ASCO: Lilly ties Retevmo to ‘dramatic’ outcomes in early-stage lung cancer with rare RET biomarker
news.snippet: In Lilly's Libretto-432 ASCO readout, Retevmo showed an 83% reduction in disease progression or death when used as an adjuvant therapy in patients with early-stage RET fusion-positive lung cancer.

news.title: ASCO: J&J breaks the prostate cancer treatment mold with fresh Erleada ph. 3 win
news.snippet: In its 2,000-patient Proteus study, J&J tested Erleada's benefits when given to certain patients with prostate cancer before and after surgery.

news.title: ASCO: Revolution Medicines confident in RAS leadership as rivals square up
news.snippet: This year’s meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology is abuzz with talk of RAS inhibitors, largely driven by Revolution Medicines’ barnburner pancreatic cancer data drop earlier this year.

news.title: ASCO: Pfizer one-ups J&J with Talzenna combo's broad castration-sensitive prostate cancer win
news.snippet: After winning a broader FDA label in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, Pfizer’s combination of Talzenna and Xtandi is claiming a phase 3 win that positions it for a wider castration-sen

news.title: ASCO: With bispecifics on its heels, Incyte positions Monjuvi combo for first-line DLBCL
news.snippet: Seeking a share of the first-line diffuse B-cell lymphoma market, Incyte believes Monjuvi’s positive phase 3 results across patient subgroups will give it an edge over the standard R-CHOP regimen and

news.title: MannKind seeks long-awaited sales boost with inhaled insulin approval for kids
news.snippet: With a pediatric approval in hand for Afrezza, MannKind may finally have the boost it needs to make an impact in the market after struggling for more than a decade with the inhaled insulin powder.

news.title: Regulatory tracker: J&J grows Tremfya label stateside; AbbVie nets EU Venclyxto expansion
news.snippet: In this tracker, Fierce Pharma is recording the regulatory progress of in-market products, including expansions into key geographies and new indications. Some of these updates may not meet the bar for

news.title: Fierce Pharma Asia—More China biotech hawkishness; Pfizer’s $10B Innovent deal; Astellas’ roadmap
news.snippet: As China-developed assets gain more attention from biopharma dealmakers, some industry watchers want to curb the trend before the U.S. loses key biotechnology capabilities. During these discussions, i

news.title: Brand-name drug prices climb after launch in US, fall abroad amid MFN push: report
news.snippet: List prices for 25 top brand-name drugs rose an average of 81% after launch in the U.S., while prices for the same medicines fell an average of 13% abroad, according to a new AARP analysis. The findin

news.title: ASCO: After Takeda’s defeat, Dizal picks up baton to take on J&J in EGFR lung cancer subtype
news.snippet: About three years after a phase 3 flop led Takeda to withdraw its Exkivity from the EGFR exon 20 lung cancer market, Dizal Pharma has data showing its small molecule drug may have what it takes to cha

news.title: Pharma urged to modernize patient support as young adult cancer rates rise
news.snippet: Pharma companies need to strengthen and modernize patient support programs to reflect rising rates of cancer in adults aged 18 to 49 years, an Omnicom Health report found.

news.title: Philips adds a spoonful of Disney sugar to ease kids’ MRI anxieties
news.snippet: Philips has integrated animated Disney characters and stories into MRI machines to make imaging scans more comforting and engaging for children.

news.title: AstraZeneca gains 2nd bladder cancer nod in key expansion for Imfinzi
news.snippet: AstraZeneca has fired another shot in its bladder cancer competition with Merck’s Keytruda, with the FDA clearing its PD-L1 inhibitor Imfinzi, used alongside standard of care, to become the first immu

news.title: Pharma leaders meet with PM Takaichi in push for Japan to retain R&D edge
news.snippet: A group of over 20 global pharma leaders met with Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to address Japan's declining share of biopharma R&D.

news.title: STAT+: A standing ovation for RevMed, and caution for Akeso
news.snippet: ASCO Day 3: A huge advance for pancreatic cancer, a let down for Akeso and Summit, and a new approach to immunotherapy.

news.title: Ultra-low doses could bring costly cancer treatments to more patients in poorer countries
news.snippet: Low-dose, low-cost immunotherapies may help patients in poorer countries access high-tech cancer treatments.

news.title: STAT+: For prostate cancer patients set on surgery, new hormone regimen may improve outcomes, study finds
news.snippet: By doing hormone therapy up front in prostate cancer cases, the hope was to shrink the relapse rate after surgery. Results were encouraging.

news.title: STAT+: Akeso and Summit’s ivonescimab extends survival in squamous cell lung cancer
news.snippet: A lung cancer drug developed in China was a highlight of ASCO but doctors want to see follow-up in a more diverse population.

news.title: STAT+: Practice-changing results reported for Revolution Medicines pancreatic cancer drug
news.snippet: Much-awaited results at ASCO show that scientists have indeed found a way to drug a "greasy ball" involved in pancreatic cancer.

news.title: STAT+: BioNTech and Pfizer tout bispecifics data at ASCO, and Pazdur sees silver lining at FDA
news.snippet: ASCO, Day 2: promising news around lung cancer, new immunotherapies, and bold words from Rick Pazdur.

news.title: STAT+: China competition, ‘destruction’ at FDA give agency chance to restructure, Rick Pazdur says
news.snippet: Rick Pazdur on the FDA: “We've had a lot of destruction here, and it doesn't mean that we have to just go back and say, well, let's rebuild it as…

news.title: STAT+: Revolution Medicines starts shipping experimental pancreatic cancer drug
news.snippet: CEO Mark Goldsmith says Revolution Medicines will submit pancreatic cancer drug daraxonrasib for FDA approval soon.

news.title: STAT+: At ASCO, positive data for Bristol in multiple myeloma and Pfizer in lung cancer

news.snippet: Pharma updates from the Pharmalot campus via the Pharmalittle newsletter

news.title: STAT+: Allogene CEO David Chang stepping down
news.snippet: Pfizer invests billions to buy Chinese drugs, WHO maps experimental response to Ebola outbreak, and other biotech news from The Readout

news.title: Millions of breast cancer patients could safely avoid chemotherapy, study suggests
news.snippet: A new DNA test could help screen whether patients require the treatment or not, according to a new international trial.

news.title: Prostate cancer screening only for 'a few thousand' high risk men
news.snippet: Only men with a dangerous genetic variant and a family history of cancer should be offered screening, say UK advisors.

news.title: Ebola risk raised to 'very high' in DR Congo
news.snippet: The head of the UN health agency says the risk in the wider region is "high", but it remains "low" at the global level.

news.title: UK scientists developing Ebola vaccine that could be ready for trials in months
news.snippet: The rare species of Ebola involved - known as Bundibugyo - kills around a third of those infected and has no proven vaccine yet.

news.title: Riskiest skin cancer cases hit UK record high
news.snippet: The number of melanoma skin cancer cases has risen above 20,000 a year for the first time in the UK.
